entries.title: On the Blessings of the Patriarchs on acts 9:8
entries.excerpt: Although he saw nothing when his eyes were opened, still he saw Christ. And it was fitting that he saw Christ present and also heard him speaking. That overshadowing is not the overshadowing of blindness by grace. Indeed, it is said to Mary, "The Holy Spirit shall come upon you, and the power of the Most High shall overshadow you."

entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 9:8
entries.excerpt: And when his eyes were opened, he saw nothing. He could not see well again unless he was first properly blinded, and excluding his own confounding wisdom, would he commit himself to faith in all things.

entries.title: HOMILY ON OUR LORD 26.2 on acts 9:8
entries.excerpt: [Saul's] impairment did not [result] from our compassionate Lord, who spoke humbly there. Rather, [it was the result] of the intense light that shone intensely here. This light was not a punishment that befell Paul on account of the things he had done. It injured him with the intensity of its rays, as he himself said.

entries.title: Homily on Acts 19 on acts 9:8
entries.excerpt: And Saul arose from the earth; and when his eyes were opened, he saw no man: but they led him by the hand, and brought him into Damascus-the spoils of the devil, his goods, as from some city, yea, some metropolis which has been taken. And the wonder of it is, the enemies and foes themselves brought him in, in the sight of all!

entries.title: Homily on Acts 20 on acts 9:8
entries.excerpt: Why did he not blind his eyes entirely? This was more wonderful, that, with his eyes open, he did not see: which was just his case in respect of the Law, until the Name of Jesus was put on him.

entries.title: Homily on Acts 19 on acts 9:8
entries.excerpt: But when he was blinded, then he saw the proofs of His sovereignty and loving kindness.

entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 9:8
entries.excerpt: He does not blind everyone except Paul alone, so that the suffering might not be thought common and accidental, but of divine providence.

entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 9:8
entries.excerpt: "Saul arose from the ground, and with his eyes open he saw no one." Paul's eyes suffered because an excess of light ordinarily acts painfully, since the eyes too have a limit. They also say that an excessive sound makes one deaf and insensible.
